Name: SQL - Query Octopus Database for Fragmentation
Description:
This step template will run a fragmentation query on your Octopus database and report the results of the tables.

If you would like to set this up as a scheduled runbook and get the results in an email, please follow these instructions:
1) Create a Send an Email step after this step in your process
2) Set the body type of that email to HTML, and the body to#{Octopus.Action[STEPNAMEHERE].Output.EmailData} 
3) Set the Run Condition of that Send an Email step to Variable, and the value to #{if Octopus.Action[STEPNAMEHERE].Output.Alert== "True"}True#{/if}. If you don't do this, you will receive an email regardless of if the threshold was hit.'

Parameters.Name: IndexFragmentSQLServer
Parameters.Label: SQL Server
Parameters.HelpText: Enter the Hostname or IP address of your server. Include \Instance if necessary.

Parameters.Name: IndexFragmentSQLPort
Parameters.Label: SQL Server Port
Parameters.HelpText: If left blank, 1433 will be used by default.

Parameters.Name: IndexFragmentFragmentation
Parameters.Label: Fragmentation Threshold
Parameters.HelpText: Input the percentage of Fragmentation you would like to be alerted for.

Parameters.Name: IndexFragmentPageCount
Parameters.Label: Page Count Threshold
Parameters.HelpText: Input the minimum page count a table must have to be considered in the results.

Parameters.Name: IndexFragmentSQLUsername
Parameters.Label: SQL Server Authentication Username
Parameters.HelpText: Please leave blank if you want to use Integrated Security.

Parameters.Name: IndexFragmentSQLPassword
Parameters.Label: SQL Server Authentication Password
Parameters.HelpText: Please leave blank if you want to use Integrated Security.
